DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Introduction
text: Introduction

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Introduction
text: The structure of CoA, as per National Municipal Accounting Manual, issued by the Government of India, Ministry of Urban Development in November 2004, is being followed and is adopted as the structure of Chart of Accounts to be followed by ULBs in West Bengal. The CoA as prescribed by the Ministry is divided into the following Heads (cid:167) Function (cid:167) Functionary (Responsibility centre/Department) (cid:167) Field (Geographical centre) (cid:167) Funds (cid:167) Accounting code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Introduction
text: The ULBs are not permitted to change the codes, except where permission has been provided, and it is compulsory to follow the prescribed coding structure.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Objective
text: Objective

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Objective
text: The various objectives of preparing a chart of accounts are (cid:167) To achieve uniformity in the operations of ULBs across West Bengal. (cid:167) To facilitate the entry of transactions in a computerised environment. (cid:167) To act as a reference for the accounting transactions to be passed. (cid:167) To generate various MIS reports.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Function Code
text: Structure of Function Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Function Code
text: The function code of the ULBs shall have three levels within it. First level under this group represents various functions both obligatory and discretionary as per the West Bengal Municipal Act, 1993 known as "Function Group". Second Level in function would represent the particular type of service under a function, known as "Function Description" and third level will represent a particular cost centre, known as "Cost Centre", which provides the service. Each level shall have a two digit code. The present prescribed function code shall have function group and function description. The function code for cost centre are not prescribed at this stage, and they shall carry a zero-value.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Function Code
text: The structure of the function code can be explained with the help of the following diagram Function-Group Function Description Cost-Center Chart of Accounts

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Function Code
text: Any transaction for Income/ Expenditure/ Asset/ Liability shall be first passed through the function code, i.e. before entering the Accounting code. Example - Expenditure on Repair and Maintenance - Roads and Pavements shall be shown under Roads and Pavements - Public works, which is one of the obligatory functions of the ULBs. The accounting code for the function code will be entered as follows 2 0 PublicWorks 2 1 Roads and Pavements 0 0 Not in use 2 0 2 1 0 0 Function-Group Function Description Cost-Center

title: Structure of the Functionary Code
text: Structure of the Functionary Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Functionary Code
text: The functionary accounting code will be a four-digit accounting code, with two levels of two-digit each. The first two digits would represent the various "departments" and the next two-digits shall represent the organisational structure within the department known as "Departmental Description".

title: Structure of the Field Code
text: Structure of the Field Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Field Code
text: The field code will be a seven digit accounting code, with three levels. The First level of two digits shall represent "Boroughs", the Second level of three digits shall represent "Wards" and the third level of two digits shall represent the slum and non-slum areas.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Field Code
text: The digits from 01-99 can be used for different Boroughs and digits from 01- 999 can be used for different wards within the same borough. The digits 10 shall be used for slum-areas and digit 20 shall be used for non-slum areas. The ULB shall do the numbering of its boroughs and wards as it is done under the law and shall maintain the same number. In case a ULB does not have boroughs/wards/slums-non slums, then the code 00 shall be used. The ULB shall do the numbering and intimate CMU of the codes it is following.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Fund Code
text: Structure of the Fund Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Fund Code
text: The fund accounting code will be a four-digit Accounting code, with two levels of two-digits each.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Fund Code
text: The Major Fund would represent the broad categorisation of Funds and Minor Fund within it represents a particular fund. Major Fund Minor Fund

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Fund Code
text: Municipal Fund will be used as Major Fund with code 01. No Minor Fund will be used currently.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of the Fund Code
text: The accounting code from 02-99 , can be used by ULBs for different funds being maintained by ULBs , after approval from Department of Municipal Affairs, Government of West Bengal.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: Structure of Accounting Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: Primary Accounting Code Each accounting code consists of seven digits. The Accounting code depicts Major Head code, Minor head code and Detailed Head code. A Sub Head code between the Minor Head and Detailed Head is although prescribed, but will not be used currently. Major Head code Minor Head code Detailed-Head code Major Head code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: Major Head code will be a three digit code; the first digit in the major head code will be an identification digit, (ID digit) which shall indicate the nature of the transaction. (cid:167) If the first digit is "1" , it would represent Revenue Income Chart of Accounts (cid:167) If the first digit is "2", it would represent Revenue Expenditure (cid:167) If the first digit is "3", it would represent Capital Receipts and Liabilities (cid:167) If the first digit is "4", it would represent Capital Expenditure and Assets

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: The next two digits in the Major Head code would be the running serial number of the respective major head code. For example if the first digit is 1 i.e. Revenue Income, then the next two digits would represent the different sources of Revenue Income i.e. Tax Revenue, Non Tax Revenue, Assigned Revenues and Compensations, etc. Minor head Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: The minor head code is a two digit code, representing further details of the major head code. As an example, if the Revenue Receipt is a Property Tax income, it will be shown as follows: 1 Major Head (Identification ID digit) Revenue Income 10 Major Head Tax Revenue 01 Minor Head Property Tax Detailed Head Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: The Detailed - Head code gives details of the Minor Head code. The Detailed - Head code is a two digits code starting from 01. As an example, if the Revenue Receipt is a Property Tax income from Residential buildings, it will be shown as follows: 1 Major Head Revenue Income 10 Major Head Tax Revenue 01 Minor Head Property Tax 01 Detailed Head Residential Buildings The following diagram illustrates operation of accounting codes for "Property tax on residential buildings". 1 1 0 0 1 0 1 Major Head Code Major Head Code Detailed Head Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: List of Primary Accounting code is attached as Annexure-2 Secondary accounting code Chart of Accounts

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Structure of Accounting Code
text: Secondary accounting code will reflect the subsidiary ledgers and other analysis ledgers. This will enable the ULBs for party-wise accounting. For example, the Contractors and Suppliers account can be maintained contractor wise at this level. A three digit code is prescribed for the subsidiary ledger of each control account. The use of secondary accounting code can be explained with the following illustration. Bill received from Mr. X, a contractor and booked as liability under Contractor Payable 3 Major Head Capital Receipts and Liabilities 50 Major Head Other Liabilities 10 Minor Head Creditors 02 Detailed Head Contractors 3 5 0 1 0 0 2 Major Head Major Head Detailed Head Secondary accounting code 0 0 1 The secondary accounting code 001 can be used for payable to a particular contractor, say Mr. X.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Identification Code for ULB
text: Identification Code for ULB

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Identification Code for ULB
text: The chart of Accounts will be same for all ULB's across West Bengal. Thus, each ULB will have a unique identification code for itself, which shall be in the following format. State Code District Code Category Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Identification Code for ULB
text: The state code will be same for all ULBs i.e. 19, State code for West Bengal. The district code would denote the district in which the ULBs are located. The category code would denote whether it is a Municipal Corporation, Municipality or a Notified Area Authority. The Code would denote the code for each ULB. The State Code will be 19 for all ULBs. The District Code has been detailed in Annexure III. The Category codes will be 10, 20 and 30 Chart of Accounts respectively for Municipal Corporation, Municipality and Notified Area Authority. The detailed identification code for each ULB is also provided in Annexure III.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: Function Code The function group codes can be changed/ added only at the National level. Any requirement for an addition to Function Group head shall be requested by the States to the Ministry of Urban Development. The committee set up by the Ministry in this regard can add new heads under Function Group.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: State government can add functional description code. All additions made shall be intimated to the committee.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The ULBs may send in their requests for additions in the Function code to the Municipal Affairs Department. Functionary code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The Department codes prescribed in the chart of accounts are mandatory for all ULBs. The ULBs can use the codes for departmental description code, after approval from Municipal Affairs Department. The Municipal Affairs Department or any other authority as may be authorised by the Municipal Affairs Department, shall prescribe the uniform codes for ULBs, after taking into account request from ULBs. Field Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The ULBs shall prepare a coding structure for their Boroughs and Wards, based on the current numbering pattern and intimate the same to the Municipal Affairs Department or any other authority as may be authorised by the Municipal Affairs Department. Chart of Accounts Fund Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The ULBs presently have a single Fund concept i.e. Municipal Fund, only this will be used presently. The accounting code from 02-99 , can be used by ULBs for different funds being maintained by ULBs , after approval from Department of Municipal Affairs, Government of West Bengal. The Municipal Affairs Department or any other authority as may be authorised by the Municipal Affairs Department, shall prescribe the uniform codes for ULBs, after taking into account request from ULBs. Primary Accounting Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The Major codes can be changed/ added only at the National Level. Any requirement for an addition to Major Head shall be requested by the States to the Ministry of Urban Development. The committee set up by the Ministry in this regard can add new heads under Major Head Code.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: Any addition to the Minor heads can be done by the State government. The State Government shall intimate all additions to the committee. If the ULBs want to make any change in the Minor Heads, they should request the same to the Municipal Affairs Department or any other authority as may be authorised by Municipal Affairs Department and only with prior approval they can be changed.

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The detailed heads prescribed are mandatory for all ULBs. The ULBs can add the new detailed heads (From 51-99) after pre-approval of the Municipal Affairs Department. The Municipal Affairs Department or any other authority as may be authorised by the Municipal Affairs Department, will review the requests from various ULBs and if consider appropriate, may prescribe it uniformly across all ULBs (Detailed codes 01-50). Secondary Accounting Code

DOC_NAME: 20. Chart of Account Accounting Manual Part_4_State Audit West Ben
title: Procedure for the Change in the Chart of Accounts
text: The ULBs will have to develop their own secondary accounting codes and use it for party-wise accounting.
